messages.content: You are OpenCat, a concise and practical coding agent. Keep the main conversation focused on the user's objective, decisions, progress, and verification. Use delegate_file_task for workspace file inspection, document rewrites, code edits, refactors, path moves, deletions, and any task that requires reading or changing file content. Do not use bash or other tools to print, cat, sed, dump, or patch file content when delegate_file_task can perform the file work. Use inspect_project for a lightweight project overview before broad changes when useful. Use bash for non-interactive git and shell inspection commands, and prefer verify_project for build, lint, typecheck, and test checks. Use start_background_process, read_background_process, probe_port, and stop_background_process for dev servers or long-running local checks. When using delegate_file_task, provide the objective, scope, constraints, and whether changes should be applied. Consume its structured result instead of asking for raw file content. Treat delegate_file_task diff, changed file list, hashes, and verification status as the source of truth for file work. After code changes, run the most relevant verification with verify_project when practical, and treat failing verification as work to diagnose before answering. Every tool call must include a concise reason and expectedResult argument explaining why the tool is needed and what result is expected. Your working context should stay focused on the user's input and the current state. Tool calls are handled out of band. After delegate_file_task completes, continue from its structured status, inspected paths, changed paths, diffs, and verification results. Do not ask for full raw file content in the main context. If more file work is needed, delegate a narrower follow-up file task.

User input:
修改docs，使其能作为一个产品的正式文档，现在文档的实现和规划还有很多问题，你需要逐一修复，并且修改文档的语气和用词，使其看起来是一个真实的用户

Subagents are available through the directly attached `agent_app_subagents` MCP server.
Use a subagent when a focused delegated task, independent review, parallel investigation, or specialist perspective would help.
Call `createSubagent` to create a temporary subagent for this run from a name, description, system prompt, required MCP tools, and optionally a base provider such as `codex` or `claude-code`. Then call `callSubagent` with the returned subagent id.
You can also call `subagent_list` to inspect attached or dynamically created subagents.
No subagents were pre-attached for this run; create one only when delegation would materially help.
Do not mention this guidance unless it is relevant to the user request.
</subagent-guidance>

function.name: delegate_file_task
function.description: Delegate workspace file reading, analysis, editing, rewrites, path operations, and file-scoped verification to an internal file-task agent. Returns structured results, changed paths, diffs, and verification signals without exposing raw file content to the main agent.

objective.description: The concrete file task to complete, including the intended outcome.

mode.description: Use inspect to understand files, plan to propose changes without writing, apply to modify files, and verify to run file-scoped checks.

scope.description: Workspace-relative files, directories, or globs that bound the task.

constraints.description: Constraints the file-task agent must preserve, such as tone, APIs, formatting, or files that must not change.

verification.description: Optional verification command or expectation for the file-task agent to consider after changes.

maxRounds.description: Maximum internal file-task rounds. Defaults to 8; maximum 18.

reason.description: Why delegating file work is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What structured result, change, or verification signal the main agent expects.

function.name: bash
function.description: Run a non-interactive bash command in the workspace. Use this for build, test, install, git, and shell inspection commands. Prefer read_file/write_file/edit_file for direct file operations.

command.description: Bash command to run with `bash -lc`. Keep it non-interactive.

directory.description: Workspace-relative working directory. Defaults to the workspace root.

timeoutMs.description: Timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to 30000; maximum 120000.

reason.description: Why this command is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What output, exit status, or side effect the agent expects from this command.

function.name: inspect_project
function.description: Inspect manifests, scripts, languages, test files, and likely verification commands for this workspace.

maxFiles.description: Maximum file paths to include. Defaults to 80.

reason.description: Why project inspection is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What project structure or command information the agent expects to learn.

function.name: verify_project
function.description: Run project verification commands. If commands are omitted, detect typecheck, lint, test, and build commands from manifests.

commands.description: Optional commands to run in order. Omit to auto-detect project checks.

directory.description: Workspace-relative working directory. Defaults to the workspace root.

stopOnFailure.description: Stop after the first failing command. Defaults to true.

reason.description: Why project verification is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What successful or failing verification signal the agent expects.

function.name: start_background_process
function.description: Start a long-running non-interactive workspace command such as a dev server and keep its logs available.

directory.description: Workspace-relative working directory. Defaults to the workspace root.

startupWaitMs.description: Milliseconds to collect startup logs before returning. Defaults to 1000.

reason.description: Why this background process is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What running service or logs the agent expects from this process.

function.name: read_background_process
function.description: Read status and recent stdout/stderr from a managed background process.

processId.description: Managed process ID returned by start_background_process.

reason.description: Why reading this process is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What process status or log evidence the agent expects.

function.name: list_background_processes
function.description: List managed background processes started by this OpenCat process.

reason.description: Why listing processes is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What process information the agent expects to learn.

function.name: stop_background_process
function.description: Stop a managed background process by sending SIGTERM, optionally followed by SIGKILL.

processId.description: Managed process ID returned by start_background_process.

force.description: Send SIGKILL after the grace period if the process is still running. Defaults to false.

graceMs.description: Milliseconds to wait after SIGTERM. Defaults to 1000.

reason.description: Why stopping this process is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What stopped process state the agent expects.

function.name: probe_port
function.description: Probe a TCP port on localhost or another host to see whether a background service is reachable.

reason.description: Why this port probe is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What service reachability signal the agent expects.

function.name: list_mcp_servers
function.description: List MCP servers installed for this workspace. Use this to see which MCP integrations are available.

reason.description: Why this tool call is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What installed MCP server information the agent expects to learn.

function.name: install_mcp_server
function.description: Install or update an MCP server for this workspace by writing its command configuration. Installed MCP tools become available on the next agent run.

name.description: Workspace-local MCP server name. Use letters, digits, underscore, dash, or dot.

command.description: Executable command for the MCP stdio server, such as npx, node, python, or an absolute binary path.

args.description: Command arguments passed without a shell, for example ['-y', '@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem', '.'].

env.description: Optional environment variables for the MCP server. Values are stored in the workspace MCP config.

cwd.description: Optional working directory for the MCP server. Relative paths resolve inside the workspace.

disabled.description: Install the server disabled. Defaults to false.

timeoutMs.description: Per-request timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to 30000.

reason.description: Why installing this MCP server is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What MCP server configuration should exist after installation.

function.name: remove_mcp_server
function.description: Remove an installed MCP server from this workspace. Its tools stop being loaded on future agent runs.

name.description: Installed MCP server name to remove.

reason.description: Why removing this MCP server is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What MCP server should no longer be installed after removal.

message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to modify the docs in this workspace to make them suitable as formal product documentation. They mention there are issues with implementation and planning that need fixing, and the tone/wording needs to be adjusted to sound like it's written for real users.

Let me start by inspecting the project structure and understanding what we're working with.

messages.content: You are a tool-result subagent. You receive raw tool output and produce a compact interpretation for the main agent. The main agent also receives a deterministic Tool evidence block generated by the runtime; do not contradict that evidence. You are not merely summarizing. Infer what the main agent needs next: relevant symbols, exact code ranges, changed behavior, stale reads, failure causes, and follow-up actions. For file reads, preserve exact paths, line numbers, and code snippets needed for editing. If the read was too broad, identify the relevant sections rather than claiming the file was changed. For write/edit/path/verification tools, explain what changed, which paths were affected, whether the change matched the tool intent, and any failure hints needed to continue. For bash results, preserve the command, working directory, exit code, and the relevant stdout or stderr lines. For read_file, report what the requested file or line range actually contains. Never say the file was written, overwritten, edited, created, deleted, or successfully modified by a read-only tool. Do not mention cache, cache hits, cache prefixes, SHA values, or prompt container labels in the state update unless the tool result is explicitly about cache diagnostics. When file content is present before the requirements, treat it plus ordered file tool-call records as the source of truth. Apply file tool-call records in sequence instead of assuming the base file already contains later modifications. Do not answer the user directly. Do not pass through irrelevant raw data. Return plain text that starts with 'Current state:'.

message.content:
Current state: The project is "claude-code-router" version 3.0.0, an Electron + React + TypeScript application using npm. The root is at `/Volumes/Data/Products/claude-code-router`. There are 255 total files (listing was truncated). Key structural observations:

- **Source code languages**: 98 `.ts` files, 45 `.tsx` (React components), 6 `.mjs` build scripts, plus Tailwind CSS, dnd-kit, baseui, styletron, recharts, lucide-react, and motion.
- **Backend/dependencies**: better-sqlite3, electron-updater, node-forge, undici, `@the-next-ai/ai-gateway`, `@the-next-ai/bot-gateway-sdk`.
- **Build system**: esbuild, electron-builder (with mac/win release configs), local entitlement plists, notarization verification scripts.
- **Documentation site** is in `./docs/`, an Astro-based site with English and Chinese content:
  - EN docs: `./docs/src/content/docs/en/index.md`, `./docs/src/content/docs/en/relay-agents-in-im-with-bots/{dingtalk,discord,feishu,line,slack,telegram,wecom,weixin-ilink}.md`
  - ZH docs: `./docs/src/content/docs/zh/index.md`, `./docs/src/content/docs/zh/bot-与-im-接力-agent/{dingtalk,discord,feishu,line,slack,telegram,wecom,weixin-ilink}.md`
  - Pages: `./docs/src/pages/index.astro`, `./docs/src/pages/en/index.astro`, `./docs/src/pages/bot-与-im-接力-agent/[platform].astro`, `./docs/src/pages/en/relay-agents-in-im-with-bots/[platform].astro`
  - Components: `DocPage.astro`, `DocsLayout.astro`, i18n content module, global CSS.
- **Blog directory** with both EN and ZH posts (project motivation and how it works, progressive disclosure, doing more with the route).
- **Assets**: icons, tray images for Electron.
- **Opencat agent configs** are present but likely not relevant.
- **Test files**: none listed (empty `testFiles` array).
- **Verification commands**: `npm run typecheck` (confidence medium), `npm run build` (confidence high).

The user wants to modify the docs to be proper product documentation — fix implementation and planning issues, adjust tone/wording to sound like a real user-facing product. Next steps should include reading the actual doc files to identify specific content issues.

Subagent interpretation:
The project is "claude-code-router" version 3.0.0, an Electron + React + TypeScript application using npm. The root is at `/Volumes/Data/Products/claude-code-router`. There are 255 total files (listing was truncated). Key structural observations:

- **Source code languages**: 98 `.ts` files, 45 `.tsx` (React components), 6 `.mjs` build scripts, plus Tailwind CSS, dnd-kit, baseui, styletron, recharts, lucide-react, and motion.
- **Backend/dependencies**: better-sqlite3, electron-updater, node-forge, undici, `@the-next-ai/ai-gateway`, `@the-next-ai/bot-gateway-sdk`.
- **Build system**: esbuild, electron-builder (with mac/win release configs), local entitlement plists, notarization verification scripts.
- **Documentation site** is in `./docs/`, an Astro-based site with English and Chinese content:
  - EN docs: `./docs/src/content/docs/en/index.md`, `./docs/src/content/docs/en/relay-agents-in-im-with-bots/{dingtalk,discord,feishu,line,slack,telegram,wecom,weixin-ilink}.md`
  - ZH docs: `./docs/src/content/docs/zh/index.md`, `./docs/src/content/docs/zh/bot-与-im-接力-agent/{dingtalk,discord,feishu,line,slack,telegram,wecom,weixin-ilink}.md`
  - Pages: `./docs/src/pages/index.astro`, `./docs/src/pages/en/index.astro`, `./docs/src/pages/bot-与-im-接力-agent/[platform].astro`, `./docs/src/pages/en/relay-agents-in-im-with-bots/[platform].astro`
  - Components: `DocPage.astro`, `DocsLayout.astro`, i18n content module, global CSS.
- **Blog directory** with both EN and ZH posts (project motivation and how it works, progressive disclosure, doing more with the route).
- **Assets**: icons, tray images for Electron.
- **Opencat agent configs** are present but likely not relevant.
- **Test files**: none listed (empty `testFiles` array).
- **Verification commands**: `npm run typecheck` (confidence medium), `npm run build` (confidence high).

The user wants to modify the docs to be proper product documentation — fix implementation and planning issues, adjust tone/wording to sound like a real user-facing product. Next steps should include reading the actual doc files to identify specific content issues.

function.name: delegate_file_task
function.description: Delegate workspace file reading, analysis, editing, rewrites, path operations, and file-scoped verification to an internal file-task agent. Returns structured results, changed paths, diffs, and verification signals without exposing raw file content to the main agent.

objective.description: The concrete file task to complete, including the intended outcome.

mode.description: Use inspect to understand files, plan to propose changes without writing, apply to modify files, and verify to run file-scoped checks.

scope.description: Workspace-relative files, directories, or globs that bound the task.

constraints.description: Constraints the file-task agent must preserve, such as tone, APIs, formatting, or files that must not change.

verification.description: Optional verification command or expectation for the file-task agent to consider after changes.

maxRounds.description: Maximum internal file-task rounds. Defaults to 8; maximum 18.

reason.description: Why delegating file work is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What structured result, change, or verification signal the main agent expects.

function.name: bash
function.description: Run a non-interactive bash command in the workspace. Use this for build, test, install, git, and shell inspection commands. Prefer read_file/write_file/edit_file for direct file operations.

command.description: Bash command to run with `bash -lc`. Keep it non-interactive.

directory.description: Workspace-relative working directory. Defaults to the workspace root.

timeoutMs.description: Timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to 30000; maximum 120000.

reason.description: Why this command is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What output, exit status, or side effect the agent expects from this command.

function.name: inspect_project
function.description: Inspect manifests, scripts, languages, test files, and likely verification commands for this workspace.

maxFiles.description: Maximum file paths to include. Defaults to 80.

reason.description: Why project inspection is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What project structure or command information the agent expects to learn.

function.name: verify_project
function.description: Run project verification commands. If commands are omitted, detect typecheck, lint, test, and build commands from manifests.

commands.description: Optional commands to run in order. Omit to auto-detect project checks.

directory.description: Workspace-relative working directory. Defaults to the workspace root.

stopOnFailure.description: Stop after the first failing command. Defaults to true.

reason.description: Why project verification is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What successful or failing verification signal the agent expects.

function.name: start_background_process
function.description: Start a long-running non-interactive workspace command such as a dev server and keep its logs available.

directory.description: Workspace-relative working directory. Defaults to the workspace root.

startupWaitMs.description: Milliseconds to collect startup logs before returning. Defaults to 1000.

reason.description: Why this background process is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What running service or logs the agent expects from this process.

function.name: read_background_process
function.description: Read status and recent stdout/stderr from a managed background process.

processId.description: Managed process ID returned by start_background_process.

reason.description: Why reading this process is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What process status or log evidence the agent expects.

function.name: list_background_processes
function.description: List managed background processes started by this OpenCat process.

reason.description: Why listing processes is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What process information the agent expects to learn.

function.name: stop_background_process
function.description: Stop a managed background process by sending SIGTERM, optionally followed by SIGKILL.

processId.description: Managed process ID returned by start_background_process.

force.description: Send SIGKILL after the grace period if the process is still running. Defaults to false.

graceMs.description: Milliseconds to wait after SIGTERM. Defaults to 1000.

reason.description: Why stopping this process is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What stopped process state the agent expects.

function.name: probe_port
function.description: Probe a TCP port on localhost or another host to see whether a background service is reachable.

reason.description: Why this port probe is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What service reachability signal the agent expects.

function.name: list_mcp_servers
function.description: List MCP servers installed for this workspace. Use this to see which MCP integrations are available.

reason.description: Why this tool call is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What installed MCP server information the agent expects to learn.

function.name: install_mcp_server
function.description: Install or update an MCP server for this workspace by writing its command configuration. Installed MCP tools become available on the next agent run.

name.description: Workspace-local MCP server name. Use letters, digits, underscore, dash, or dot.

command.description: Executable command for the MCP stdio server, such as npx, node, python, or an absolute binary path.

args.description: Command arguments passed without a shell, for example ['-y', '@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem', '.'].

env.description: Optional environment variables for the MCP server. Values are stored in the workspace MCP config.

cwd.description: Optional working directory for the MCP server. Relative paths resolve inside the workspace.

disabled.description: Install the server disabled. Defaults to false.

timeoutMs.description: Per-request timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to 30000.

reason.description: Why installing this MCP server is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What MCP server configuration should exist after installation.

function.name: remove_mcp_server
function.description: Remove an installed MCP server from this workspace. Its tools stop being loaded on future agent runs.

name.description: Installed MCP server name to remove.

reason.description: Why removing this MCP server is needed for the current task. Keep it concise.

expectedResult.description: What MCP server should no longer be installed after removal.
